title: Ensure that a limit is set on pod PIDs

input_row.title: Ensure that a limit is set on pod PIDs
input_row.description: Ensure that the Kubelet sets limits on the number of PIDs that can be created by pods running on the node.
input_row.rationale: By default pods running in a cluster can consume any number of PIDs, potentially exhausting the resources available on the node. Setting an appropriate limit reduces the risk of a denial of service attack on cluster nodes. Impact: Setting this value will restrict the number of processes per pod. If this limit is lower than the number of PIDs required by a pod it will not operate.
input_row.audit: Review the Kubelet's start-up parameters for the value of --pod-max-pids, and check the Kubelet configuration file for the PodPidsLimit . If neither of these values is set, then there is no limit in place.
input_row.remediation: Decide on an appropriate level for this parameter and set it, either via the --pod-max- pids command line parameter or the PodPidsLimit configuration file setting. Default Value: By default the number of PIDs is not limited. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pid-limiting/#pod-pid-limits 4.3 kube-proxy Recommendations relating to the kube-proxy component.
input_row.impact: Setting this value will restrict the number of processes per pod. If this limit is lower than the number of PIDs required by a pod it will not operate.
input_row.references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pid-limiting/#pod-pid-limits 4.3 kube-proxy Recommendations relating to the kube-proxy component.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubelet_pod_pids_limit_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the Kubelet configuration on each worker node to verify that a limit is set on the number of PIDs that can be created by pods.
- Specifically, check for the presence and value of either:
  - The `--pod-max-pids` command-line argument in the Kubelet process, or
  - The `podPidsLimit` field in the Kubelet configuration file (typically `/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml`).
- Use the following methods:
  - For clusters with access to node configurations via the Kubernetes API (e.g., using the `kubeletconfig` API or via DaemonSet to inspect node files):
    - Retrieve the Kubelet configuration file and inspect the `podPidsLimit` field.
    - Example: `kubectl get node <node-name> -o json | jq '.status.nodeInfo.kubeletVersion'` (to determine version compatibility), then use a privileged DaemonSet to read `/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml` and check for `podPidsLimit`.
  - For clusters where Kubelet is configured via command-line flags:
    - Use a privileged DaemonSet to inspect the running Kubelet process and check for the `--pod-max-pids` flag.
    - Example: `ps aux | grep kubelet` inside a privileged pod.
- Pass Criteria:
  - At least one of the following is true for every node:
    - `podPidsLimit` is set to a positive integer in the Kubelet config file, OR
    - `--pod-max-pids` is set to a positive integer in the Kubelet process arguments.
- Fail Criteria:
  - Neither `podPidsLimit` nor `--pod-max-pids` is set, or the value is missing/zero/unlimited.
- The check should be performed on all worker nodes in the cluster.
- Reference: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/policy/pid-limiting/#pod-pid-limits

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.9.0 PDF
